body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_5)
Regime data: VIX 16.42 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 72.7%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime signals moderate risk appetite with VIX at 16.42, elevated but still below the 3-month implied volatility of 19.32, and HY OAS at 269 bps, reflecting cautious credit conditions amid a firm US Treasury curve (2Y at 4.16%, 10Y at 4.54%) and a steady dollar (DXY 101.08). SPY is pressured lower at $752.11 with negative sentiment (-1), weighed down by geopolitical tensions as Iran launches new strikes, setting a bearish near-term tone for broad equities. Conversely, NVDA ($207.89) and AMZN ($246.54) maintain bullish sentiment (+1) fueled by structural growth drivers: NVDA benefits from the AI infrastructure boom pushing Taiwan suppliers toward U.S. manufacturing expansion, while AMZN’s outlook is buoyed by MercadoLibre’s forecasted entry into the $50 billion revenue club by 2027, underscoring robust e-commerce growth. The key catalyst to watch today is the Treasury balance release, which could influence short-term rates and risk sentiment ahead of this week’s critical CPI and PPI data; any surprise inflation prints could derail the fragile rally. The primary risk building is geopolitical escalation from Iran, which could exacerbate market volatility and stall risk assets, especially given the already cautious breadth at 72.7%. System remains in warmup mode with 21 daily runs before IV rank filters engage, so trade signals are not yet fully live.

news.title: Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Prediction: MercadoLibre Will Join Amazon, Walmart, and Costco in the $50 Billion Revenue Club by 2027
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
AI Infrastructure Boom Drives Taiwan Suppliers Toward U.S. Manufacturing Expansion
SK Hynix Makes its US Debut: Which ETFs Offer Exposure?
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes
Regular water isn't enough: The corporate gold rush for ‘functional hydration’
Fed Chair Warsh faces the hot seat as Congress presses for a read on the economy
‘Day-to-day dread’ haunts frustrated job seekers in era of low hiring

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), and Wells Fargo (WFC) reporting on July 14, followed by Morgan Stanley (MS) on July 15. Investors will focus on loan growth, trading revenue, and credit quality amid the current macro regime of moderate volatility (VIX 16.42) and elevated high-yield spreads (HY OAS 269 bps). Positive surprises could buoy financials and provide a tailwind to SPY, while weakness might pressure risk sentiment and weigh on cyclical names like AMZN. Healthcare heavyweight UnitedHealth (UNH) reports on July 16 with bullish expectations, offering a potential defensive offset.
narratives.next_week: Next week shifts attention to consumer discretionary and industrials with Tesla (TSLA) on July 22 and a cluster of industrial and materials names including Raytheon Technologies (RTX), Honeywell (HON), and Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) through July 23. TSLA’s results will be a key barometer for growth and innovation sentiment, influencing tech-related growth stocks such as NVDA and AMZN. Industrial and materials earnings will provide insight into global demand and supply chain dynamics, which could ripple through cyclicals and impact SPY’s sector composition. Communication Services and Real Estate earnings from AT&T (T) and Crown Castle (CCI) add further nuance to the sector rotation narrative.
narratives.next_month: Looking beyond two weeks, the calendar becomes more event-heavy with the July 30 FOMC meeting and July 31 PCE inflation data dominating market focus. The Fed is overwhelmingly expected to hold rates at 3.50%, but any hawkish tone could pressure equities broadly, especially growth-oriented names like NVDA and AMZN. Key earnings from Qualcomm (QCOM), Chevron (CVX), and AbbVie (ABBV) will provide sector-specific catalysts, while August’s NFP and CPI reports set the stage for late summer market positioning. The risk-off bias implied by inflation and labor data expectations suggests a cautious stance on SPY heading into August.
